# Green2Gro

> Green2Gro designs, builds and installs Controlled Environment Agriculture (CEA) and aeroponic vertical farming systems, engineered in South Africa for growers worldwide.

Green2Gro is a South African company (Green2Gro Vertical Systems (Pty) Ltd) that builds precision-engineered CEA chambers using true aeroponic misting technology — not hydroponics. Roots are suspended in open air and fed an ultra-fine, precisely timed nutrient mist, with no standing water, which avoids the mould risk and inconsistent results associated with basic hydroponic tray systems.

## Key facts

- Technology: aeroponic (not hydroponic) — roots in open air, misted with nutrient solution
- Water use: up to 95% less than conventional farming
- Yield: up to 10x higher than traditional farming in the same floor area
- Growing days: 365 days/year, any climate
- Pesticide use: 0%
- Typical ROI: 12–18 months after commissioning
- Operating countries: South Africa, Namibia, Canada; serving clients worldwide
- Founder: Nick Kemp, with two decades of hands-on experience in germinated-grain feed systems since 2005; featured as an industry expert in Landbou.com's "Vra vir Faffa" (Ask the Expert) column in 2018

## Products

- Controlled Environment Agriculture (CEA) chambers — custom-built, climate-controlled grow rooms
- Aeroponic vertical farming systems
- Livestock fodder chambers — grow sprouted wheatgrass, barley and oats as living feed in 6–8 days for dairy cattle, beef cattle, sheep, poultry, pigs, and game/wildlife
- Human-consumption crops — microgreens, exotic mushrooms, leafy greens and herbs, wheatgrass and sprouts
- Installation, staff training, remote monitoring and ongoing maintenance
- Sustainability consulting — solar integration, water recirculation, closed-loop nutrient delivery

## Fodder science (livestock feed)

Germinating barley, oats or wheatgrass seed increases digestibility from roughly 30% (dry seed) to 90–95% (sprouted), raises protein content from about 8% to 21%, and increases biomass weight by up to 620% within 8 days. Documented results across Green2Gro installations include an 8–13% increase in daily milk yield for dairy cattle.
